# Chapter 27 Hard Drop Tasks

The following tasks are available for completing the Hard Drop portion of the audit:

· Actual Hard Drop-Retrieves and displays hard-drop information.

· Hard Drop Manual Entry-Allows for manual entry of hard-drop details (also referred
to as Hard Drop Edit).

· Correct Hard Drop-Provides an option to correct hard-drop errors.

· Correct Hard Drop Variances-Provides an option to correct hard-drop information.


## To access the Hard Drop tasks:

Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.




![Figure 27-1 User Tasks List-the Hard Drop Tasks](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/299.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="299" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 27 Hard Drop Tasks" -->


### 27.1 Actual Hard Drop

Use the Actual Hard Drop function to retrieve hard drop data from a disk, enter the data
manually, or confirm no drop for the audit period.


#### To retrieve the actual hard drop:

1\. If necessary, select Open Period from the toolbar. The current audit period appears.

2\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

3\. Click Actual Hard Drop. The hard drop is retrieved and processed (if previously
posted).

OR

If a hard drop does not exist for the accounting period, the Hard Drop Selection window
appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/300.1.png)




a. Select one of the following:

· From Disk-Select in the case of a network failure, if the actual drop was saved to
a disk.

· Enter Drop Manually-Select if the hard-drop machine experienced a failure
OR if information transfer did not occur.

· No Drop Today-Select if no hard drop was done for the accounting day.

b. Click OK. A confirmation appears once the hard-drop information is retrieved.

4\. Click Done. The retrieved data appears.

5\. Click OK. The Enter Hard Drop Manual Meters window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/300.2.png)




6\. Click Exceptions to display the any hard drops exceeding the percentage of variance
between actual and metered hard drop.


### 27.2 Adjusting Hard Drops

When working on hard drop audit, auditors can assign orphan buckets to orphan machines.
For example, while correcting hard-drop errors, the auditor views a list of orphan buckets along
with a list of orphan machines. The auditor clicks on one of the orphan buckets, drags it over a
chosen machine, and drops it onto that machine. The orphan bucket is applied to that machine.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="300" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 27 Hard Drop Tasks" -->


#### 27.2.1 Correcting Hard-Drop Errors




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/301.1.png)




This option should only be accessed after getting the Actual Hard Drop for the
period.


## To correct hard-drop errors:

1\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

2\. Click Correct Hard Drop Errors. The Correct Hard Drop Errors window opens.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/301.2.png)




3\. Drag the orphan buckets to the orphan machines.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/301.3.png)




### To unassign a drop from a machine:

1\. Right-click a drop item on the Correct Hard Drop Errors window.

2\. Select Unassign Drop. The drop is unassigned from the machine.

3\. Click Finish Drop Errors after all drops are assigned.


### 27.2.2 Accepting Hard-Drop Variances

To accept a hard-drop variance:

1\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

2\. Click Correct Hard Drop Variances. The Correct Hard Drop Variances window
opens.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="301" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 27 Hard Drop Tasks" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/302.1.png)




3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the machines shown.

4\. Select a machine, then click Accept if the variance is within established guidelines. A
confirmation prompt appears.

5\. Click Yes. The variance is accepted, and the Correct Hard Drop Variance window no
longer displays the drop.


### 27.2.3 Swapping Hard-Drop Variances

Swap a variance if, after reviewing hard-drop variances, the actual drops for two machines are
incorrectly posted.


#### To swap hard-drop variances between machines:

1\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

2\. Click Correct Hard Drop Variances. The Correct Hard Drop Variances window
opens.

3\. Select a machine, or press and hold CTRL and click to select multiple machines.

4\. Right-click a selected machine, then select Swap Actual Drop to repost the money to
the correct machine. A confirmation prompt appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/302.2.png)




i
An information message appears if the machines selected are not the same
denomination.

5\. Click Yes. The assignment is saved.


### 27.2.4 Unaccepting Hard-Drop Variances


#### To unaccept a hard-drop variance:

1\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

2\. Click Correct Hard Drop Variances. The Correct Hard Drop Variances window
opens.

3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the machines shown.

4\. Select a machine, then click Unaccept if the variance is not within established guidelines.
A confirmation prompt appears.

5\. Click Yes. The variance is no longer accepted.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="302" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 27 Hard Drop Tasks" -->


# 27.2.5 Adjusting Hard Drops

To adjust a hard drop:

1\. Click Hard Drop in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Hard Drop tasks appear.

2\. Click Correct Hard Drop Variances. The Correct Hard Drop Variances window
opens.

3\. Select All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the machines shown.

4\. Select a machine, then click Actuals. The Hard Drop Edit window opens.


<table>
<tr>
<th colspan="3">Hard Drop Edit x</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2">Machine Number:</th>
<th colspan="2"></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>100000</td>
<td>Save</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Coin Drop Amount:</td>
<td>150</td>
<td>Delete</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Metered Amount:</td>
<td>0.00</td>
<td>Done</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Variance Amount:</td>
<td>150.00</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</table>




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/303.1.png)




i
Another way to adjust a hard drop is to click Hard Drop on the User Tasks list
and then select Hard Drop Manual Entry. This requires an entry in the Machine
Number field to continue.

5\. Enter the amount actually dropped in the Coin Drop Amount field.

6\. Click Save to confirm the adjustment, or click Done to cancel.


# 27.2.6 Adjusting Hard Meters

A hard meter reading may be adjusted to balance a hard drop variance for an individual
machine. This is not recommended.
